texto: Today's politicians are like policemen on traffic duty. They are just dealing with traffic right in front of them. Yet they want to be the Superintendent based on that alone. The politicians and the bigwigs in finance have no ambition. There are too many who confuse the power that they got by their position as their own ability.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jirō Shirasu: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a tempo. O trecho “Today's politicians are like policemen on traffic duty. They are just dealing with traffic right …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, ambition, vida pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e vida.
